subject: Sociology
title: Labeling Theory
description: A quick win: understand Labeling Theory.

hook.text: A teenager shoplifts once. If no one finds out, life goes on. If she's caught and labeled, her world changes.

buildup.text: Howard Becker argued that deviance isn't the act — it's the label society attaches to the person who did it.

discovery.text: Once labeled deviant, a person faces stigma that closes doors: jobs, relationships, and self-image shift.

twist.text: Labels create secondary deviance. Being treated as a criminal pushes people deeper into criminal identity.

climax.text: The criminal justice system doesn't just respond to crime — it creates criminals through the labeling process.

punchline.text: Call someone a criminal long enough and they become one.

quiz.question: What is the key insight of labeling theory?
options: Deviance is created by the social reaction, not just the act
